ccel.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

title: To Andrew.

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: On hearing that your Glory had been severely afflicted with grief and sickness, I condoled with you exceedingly.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But learning presently that the malady had entirely left you, I soon turned my sorrow into joy, and returned great thanks to Almighty God for that He smote that He might heal, afflicted that He might lead to true joys.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For hence it is written, Whom the Lord loveth he chasteneth, and scourgeth every son whom he receiveth (Heb. xii.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Hence the Truth in person says, My Father is the husbandman, and every branch in me that beareth not fruit, he will take away; but every branch that beareth fruit, he will purge it, that it may bring forth more fruit (John xv.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For the unfruitful branch is taken away, because a sinner is utterly rooted up.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But the fruitful branch is said to be purged, because it is cut down by discipline that it may be brought to more abundant grace.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For so the grain of the ears of corn, beaten with the threshing instrument, is stript of its awn and chaff.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: So the olives, pressed in the oil-press, flow forth into the fatness of oil.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: So the bunches of grapes pounded with the heels, liquify into wine.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Rejoice, therefore, good man, for that in this thy scourge and this thy advancement thou seest that thou art loved by the Eternal Judge.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Furthermore, I beg that my daughter Gloriosa, your wife, be greeted in my name.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Now may Almighty God keep you under heavenly protection, and comfort you both now with abundance of gifts and hereafter with the retribution of reward.
